questions: Illustrator er pen tool kivabe use kore
answers: Adobe Illustrator is a vector graphics editor used for creating logos, icons, illustrations, and print designs. Unlike Photoshop, Illustrator uses mathematical paths so graphics scale infinitely without losing quality.
Illustrator holo vector design er jonno — logo, icon, illustration banate Illustrator use kora hoy. Joto baro korben toto clear thakbe, pixel e vangbe na.
Illustrator is best for logos, icons, typography, infographics, and print-ready designs. Master the Pen tool, Pathfinder, and Shape Builder to become proficient. Start with simple shapes and work up.
The key difference: Photoshop works with pixels (raster) while Illustrator works with vectors. Use Illustrator for anything that needs to scale — logos, business cards, banners, and illustrations.
